Title: Sucrose synthase activity is not required for cellulose biosynthesis in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: SUS1; SUS2; SUS3; SUS4; SUS5; SUS6
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell wall (cellulose); rosette leaves quantified for UDP-glucose
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cellulose is the major structural polysaccharide of primary and secondary cell walls; UDP-glucose is the immediate glucosyl donor for cellulose and other matrix polysaccharide synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of four or all six SUS genes does not reduce cellulose content or cause growth/cell wall defects; instead UDP-glucose levels increase in rosette leaves.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Quadruple and sextuple sus mutants lacked visible growth defects under normal conditions. 2. No collapsed xylem, hypocotyl elongation defect, or reduction in stem/leaf cellulose content was detected. 3. Rosette leaf UDP-glucose increased by about 10.6% in susquad and about 21.0%/22.4% in two sextuple mutant alleles, refuting the requirement of SUS for cellulose biosynthesis in Arabidopsis.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions in long day or short day photoperiod; etiolated seedling assay in darkness.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sucrose synthase (glycosyltransferase family enzyme involved in reversible sucrose cleavage)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite analysis by LC-MS/MS for UDP-glucose; no accession number reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No growth penalty under standard conditions in this study; discussion notes prior reports that sus1sus4 mutants show growth defects under hypoxia, implying stress-conditional importance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic analysis of quadruple and sextuple mutants; cellulose quantification (Updegraff/anthrone assay); stem anatomy microscopy; etiolated hypocotyl assay; targeted LC-MS/MS quantification of UDP-glucose.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SUS is not an effective standalone target to manipulate cellulose production in Arabidopsis under normal conditions; engineering cellulose/UDP-glucose flux should consider alternative pathways rather than relying on SUS suppression or enhancement.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats the Arabidopsis SUS family collectively as the experimental focal genes. The main conclusion is negative causality: SUS activity is dispensable for cellulose biosynthesis under the tested conditions, likely due to compensatory alternative UDP-glucose-producing pathways.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UDP-glucose is a central activated sugar donor for cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The starchless pgm mutant shows reduced rosette leaf UDP-glucose at the end of the night and serves as a positive control validating the UDP-glucose assay.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pgm was used as an assay control rather than the main research subject. 2. Reduced UDP-glucose in pgm agreed with published results. 3. This corroborated the LC-MS/MS method used to quantify UDP-glucose.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: End of dark period in short-day-grown plants.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite analysis by LC-MS/MS for UDP-glucose; no accession number reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant comparison and targeted LC-MS/MS metabolite quantification.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited in this paper because pgm is only a control and not a core experimental target for nutrient-trait engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a control/background gene in Results, not a primary core gene affecting the paper's main cellulose conclusion.
